rulings.Case Title: DEMAS YAN VS. LI MING LEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Tony Fu Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Void Order Of August 3, 2010 Granting Plaintiff Cheuk Tin Yan'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs, And To Deem Or Delcare Demas Yan To Be An Alter Ego Of Legal Recovery, Llc; Memo Of Points And Authorities In Support Pursuant To Ccp 473 And Alter Ego Doctrine
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 23, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT TONY FU'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Void Order Of August 3, 2010 Granting Plaintiff Cheuk Tin Yan'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs, And To Deem Or Delcare Demas Yan To Be An Alter Ego Of Legal Recovery, Llc; Memo Of Points And Authorities In Support Pursuant To Ccp 473 And Alter Ego Doctrine. Defendant Tony Fu's motion to set aside the order of August 3, 2010 awarding fees and costs to now deceased plaintiff Cheuk Tin Yan is granted. No opposition filed. The August 3, 2010 order is now set aside. Mr. Fu's request to deem or declare Demas Yan an alter ego of Legal Recovery is denied without prejudice to being refiled. Since this case has been dismissed in 2011 and Legal Recovery never appeared in the lawsuit, there is no need or purpose in addressing this request in this case at this time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Mr. Fu is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 23, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT KAMAL BHAKTA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Jointly And Severally Against Plaintiff Netfortris Acquisition Co., Inc. And Its Attorneys Of Record Duyen Nguyen And Dtn Law Group In The Amount Of $4,716.25. The hearing will be on August 8, 2018. Mr. Bhakta has contested the tentative ruling which was orally told to counsel on July 20, 2018. The tentative ruling is: Defendant Kamal Bhakta's motion for sanctions is denied. rulings.Case Title: JAMES WEST VS. PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 23, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANTS PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD, SAN MATEO COUNTY TRANSIT DISTRICT, AND TRANSIT AMERICA SERVICES INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Peninsula Corridor Joint Powers Board, San Mateo County Transit District and Transit America Services Inc.'s demurrer to all seven causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff James West is: a) sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract against Samtrans and TASI and the third cause of action for dangerous condition of public property against all three defendants; b) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract against Caltrain, the second cause of action for negligence against Caltrain and TASI, and the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action for violations of the Bane, Ralph and Unruh Acts against all three defendants; c) overruled as to the second cause of action for negligence against Samtrans and the seventh cause of action for unfair business practices against all three defendants. The contract shows that it was between Mr. West and Caltrain and thus there can be no breach of contract liability against Samtrans and TASI who are not parties to the contract. Mr. West is granted leave to allege facts why the waiver of liability provision in the contract is invalid and does not bar his contract claim against Caltrain and his negligence claims against Caltrain and TASI. The negligence claim against Samtrans and the unfair business practices claim against all defendants are adequately alleged. The dangerous property claim fails as to all three defendants because Mr. West has not alleged, nor does it appear that he can reasonably do so, that some physical defect of public property posed a danger to his person as opposed to his property. To the extent that the dangerous property claim is based on danger to Mr. West's property, it is duplicative of his negligence claim and not separately actionable as a dangerous condition of public property. The Bane, Ralph and Unruh Act claims are not supported by sufficient ultimate facts to show that any of the defendants violated those statutes. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TIBOR ATTILA SZABO VS. CARLENE LAUGHLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 23, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT CARLENE LAUGHLIN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash. Defendant Carlene Laughlin's motion to quash is granted. "[A] defendant has an absolute right to demand that process be issued against him in a manner prescribed by law. As a general rule, however, the provisions of the rules governing service of process are to be liberally construed." (Mannesmann DeMag, Ltd. v. Sup. Crt. (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 1118, 1123.) Plaintiff Tibor Szabo fails to show that he served Ms. Laughlin in a manner in substantial compliance with the requirements of the Code of Civil Procedure. The proof of service filed on May 29, 2018 does not clearly explain the mode of service and there is insufficient evidence showing personal or substitute service. Mr. Szabo's request to serve Ms. Laughlin's counsel is denied. There is no authority that would allow such an order under the present circumstances. Applications to serve by publication are to be made in department 206 per SF Local Rule 3.1C(2). Mr. Szabo must make a separate motion that complies with CRC 3.1324 to amend the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Ms. Laughlin is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
